author: y4rr
content: Three edits to ~/.claude/skills/rust-gpu-discipline/: (1) SKILL.md gains Step 0 'Probe the environment' before Step 1, with five concrete probe commands (nvidia-smi, /usr/local/cuda check, .cargo/config.toml CUDARC pin, cuda-feature build, cuda-feature test --no-run) and a meta-rule: any 'I can't verify this because [X]' must have [X] measured, not assumed; (2) anti-patterns.md gains #11 'The phantom constraint' using the literal example I exhibited this session ('WSL2 here doesn't expose a GPU' written from a session with a 24GB RTX 3090 idle and ready); (3) verification-script.md gains a Step 0 probe block at the top with the same commands as a copy-paste oneliner. Together: the skill no longer leaves room for self-fabricated 'I can't run the verification on this box' exits — the probe makes that claim a measurable assertion.
